PlatformHero.heading: Carbon management platform
PlatformHero.description: Discover HanaLoop's carbon management platform Hana.eco and our range of solutions.

PlatformOverview.heading: Hana.eco Carbon Accounting Solution
PlatformOverview.subheading: Systematically measure, report, and verify greenhouse gas emissions to meet global disclosure obligations.
PlatformOverview.whatBody: Carbon accounting is the system by which companies measure, record, and report all greenhouse gases they directly emit or indirectly generate. It follows the GHG Protocol international standard and is divided into Scope 1 (direct emissions), Scope 2 (purchased energy), and Scope 3 (entire value chain).
PlatformOverview.imageAlt: Port landscape related to carbon accounting

PlatformWhyNeed.whySubheading: Find out why carbon accounting is essential.
PlatformWhyNeed.imageAlt: Power plant with emission clouds background

cards.title: Regulatory Compliance
cards.body: Respond to domestic regulations such as K-ETS and Target Management, and meet global disclosure obligations including IFRS S1·S2, CDP, and GRI

cards.title: Business Decision-Making
cards.body: Use emissions data to set reduction targets, reduce costs, and manage climate risk

CbamOverview.heading: Hana.eco CBAM Solution
CbamOverview.subheading: For companies exporting to the EU, carbon data for your products is now a competitive advantage.
CbamOverview.whatBody: The EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) imposes carbon costs on the embedded emissions of products imported into the EU. Exporting companies using materials such as steel and aluminum must precisely manage their products' carbon data.
CbamOverview.solutionBody:
Export Competitiveness
CBAM certificate costs directly affect product profitability.
The more precisely you manage carbon data, the more unnecessary costs you can reduce.

Regulatory Obligation
EU-exporting companies are required to mandatorily report the embedded emissions per product unit.
Non-compliance risks export restrictions and penalties.
CbamOverview.imageAlt: Port logistics scene related to CBAM

steps.heading: 1. Assessment and Consulting
items: Assess collection status and process gaps

steps.heading: 3. Meter Installation and Integration
items: Discuss ERP/MES usage and provide connectors

steps.heading: 4. Transition
items: Train operators to internalize the process

CbamInsightCards.heading: Insights from HanaLoop to Understand CBAM

items.title: EU Omnibus Package
items.subtitle: Overview of the CBAM Reform Proposal
items.body: The European Commission announced a simplification proposal for the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) as part of the 2025 Omnibus Package. The reform aims to streamline procedures and make compliance more efficient.

items.title: EU Omnibus Package
items.subtitle: CBAM Reform Proposal Explained
items.body: This document is a translation of the material published on the official CBAM website on December 17, 2024. Companies operating in the steel, aluminum, and other metals industries are encouraged to review the latest updates.

items.title: Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)
items.subtitle: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
items.body: This document is a translation of the material published on the official CBAM website on October 24, 2024. Companies in the steel, aluminum, and other metals sectors are advised to familiarize themselves with the updates.

items.title: Responding to EU CBAM
items.subtitle: with Hana.eco — Fast and Efficiently
items.body: The European Commission announced a simplification proposal for CBAM as part of the 2025 Omnibus Package. The reform is designed to streamline procedures and reduce the compliance burden for exporters to the EU.

items.title: Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)
items.subtitle: What to Include in Your Report
items.body: This document is a translation of the material published on the official CBAM website on December 17, 2024. Businesses in the steel, aluminum, and related metals industries are encouraged to stay up to date with the latest requirements.

items.title: CBAM Consulting & Solution Onboarding
items.subtitle: Q&A from Our Customers
items.body: This document is a translation of the material published on the official CBAM website on October 24, 2024. Companies in the steel, aluminum, and other metals sectors are advised to review the updates carefully.

HanaAiOverview.sectionAriaLabel: Hana.eco AI — AI that transforms carbon data into action
HanaAiOverview.heading: Hana.eco AI — AI that transforms carbon data into action
HanaAiOverview.subheading: AI automates complex carbon regulation interpretation and data analysis. Experience faster, more accurate decision-making.
HanaAiOverview.itemOneBody: Hana.eco AI is an AI-powered carbon management assistant that automates carbon data analysis, regulatory interpretation, and report writing. It learns from vast carbon regulatory data and corporate emissions data to help practitioners make faster and more accurate decisions.

items.title: Data Anomaly Detection
body: Accuracy is one of the core principles in carbon accounting.
As massive emissions activity data is collected and managed, anomalies can occur due to many factors and reduce data reliability.
Hana.eco detects these anomalies to effectively preserve data accuracy.
items.bgAlt: Background for data anomaly detection feature

items.title: Cost Reduction and Profit Optimization
items.body: Today, carbon directly represents financial value. In ETS, CBAM, and other regimes, accurate emissions forecasting helps companies maximize profits and reduce costs.
items.extraTitle: Reduction Impact Analysis via Emissions Forecasting
extraBody: Hana.eco uses AI to forecast emissions and analyze reduction effects from operational changes, supporting strategic business decisions.

items.title: Climate Regulation Response Through Carbon Management
body: Effective carbon management requires close linkage between domain knowledge and business strategy.
This enables optimal strategy planning and fast response to evolving regulations.
extraBody: Hana.eco leverages generative AI and NLP so even non-experts can understand complex regulations and respond efficiently.
items.bgAlt: Background for Carbon Buddy AI assistant feature

PcfOverview.heading: Hana.eco PCF/LCA Solution
PcfOverview.subheading: Calculate and strategically reduce the carbon impact of each product on the planet with precision.
PcfOverview.title: What is PCF/LCA?
PcfOverview.lead: Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) is an indicator that quantifies greenhouse gas emissions across the entire lifecycle — from raw material extraction → production → distribution → use → disposal.
PcfOverview.body: Calculated based on ISO 14067 and GHG Protocol international standards.

PcfWhyNeeded.title: Why is PCF/LCA management needed?
PcfWhyNeeded.itemOneBody: Product-level carbon data is required by export regulations such as CBAM; ISO 14067-based calculation ensures reliability.
PcfWhyNeeded.itemTwoBody: Identify carbon-intensive factors in processes, materials, and the supply chain to enable concrete reduction actions.

PcfMainFeatures.title: Key Features
PcfMainFeatures.subtitle: LRQA dual conformity certification for Emissions Trading Scheme and EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism

SupplyChainOverview.sectionAriaLabel: Hana.eco Supply Chain PCF Solution Introduction
SupplyChainOverview.heading: Hana.eco Supply Chain PCF Solution
SupplyChainOverview.subheading: Collect carbon data from suppliers and transparently manage the carbon footprint across the entire value chain.
SupplyChainOverview.itemOneBody: Supply Chain PCF tracks and manages carbon emissions across the entire value chain — from raw material supply to logistics and distribution — beyond direct production activities. It collects actual measured data from suppliers based on GHG Protocol Scope 3 standards to improve calculation accuracy.
SupplyChainOverview.itemTwoTitle: Why is Supply Chain PCF management needed?
SupplyChainOverview.itemTwoBody:
Strengthening Scope 3 Disclosure Requirements
Reporting requirements for value chain emissions are increasingly strengthened in ISSB S2, CDP, CSDDD, and others.

Responding to Customer and Buyer Demands
Cases where large companies and global buyers require suppliers to submit carbon data are rapidly increasing.

members.name: 김혜연 Hyeyeon Kim
members.bio: Graduated in Physics from Ewha Womans University and Image Information Processing from Korea University, she leads HanaLoop's carbon neutrality strategy and digital carbon management platforms based on her experience at Samsung Electronics and in IT consulting. She specializes in CBAM, Scope 3, ESG carbon management solutions, and climate regulation strategies.

members.name: 안영석 Young-Suk Ahn Park
members.bio: Holding a Master's degree in Software Engineering from Carnegie Mellon University and a Computer Science background from Korea University, he has over 20 years of experience as a software architect at global companies including Walt Disney, Pearson, and Wayfair. He currently leads HanaLoop's carbon management SaaS and digital MRV platform development.

members.name: 안새애 Sae Ahn
members.bio: Graduated from Tulane University Law School and licensed as a New York attorney, she has extensive experience in international legal affairs and compliance at Samsung Electronics, LG Electronics, and Microsoft Korea. At HanaLoop, she advises on CBAM and global climate regulations.

members.name: 이평휘 PyoungHwe Lee
members.bio: Holding a Master's degree in Metallurgical Engineering from Inha University, he has experience in metal refining technologies and manufacturing process improvement at Dongbu Metal and KPCM. At HanaLoop, he supports CBAM emissions calculation and carbon reduction strategies for steel and aluminum industries.

members.name: Dionysios Tsouklakis
members.bio: Graduated in Environmental Engineering from the University of Western Macedonia, Greece, with previous experience at Aurora Energy Research and Vertis Environmental Finance in EU ETS and global carbon market analysis. He leads EU CBAM research and carbon management solution development at HanaLoop.

members.name: 김현일 Hyeon-il Kim
members.bio: Graduated in Architectural Engineering from Yonsei University, he supports CBAM activity data analysis, supply chain onboarding, Scope 3 calculation, and carbon accounting systems based on his experience in industrial and architectural data analysis.

PrivacyPage.title: HanaLoop Privacy Policy
PrivacyPage.intro: HanaLoop Co., Ltd. ("HanaLoop") establishes and discloses this Privacy Policy in accordance with Article 30 of the Personal Information Protection Act to protect data subjects' personal information and to handle related grievances promptly and smoothly.

sections.title: Article 1 (Purpose of Processing Personal Information)
paragraphs: HanaLoop processes personal information for the following purposes. Processed personal information is not used for purposes other than those listed below. If the purpose of use changes, HanaLoop will take necessary measures such as obtaining separate consent in accordance with Article 18 of the Personal Information Protection Act.
bullets: A. Website membership registration and management: confirmation of intent to join, user identification/authentication, membership maintenance, prevention of unauthorized use, notices, and grievance handling
B. Service provision: greenhouse gas activity/environmental data processing services, identity verification, payment/settlement, and collaboration infrastructure
C. Grievance handling: identity verification of complainants, confirmation of complaints, contact/notice for fact-finding, and notification of results
D. HanaLoop personal information processing tasks: [purpose according to processing task]

sections.title: Article 2 (Processing and Retention Period)
paragraphs: HanaLoop processes and retains personal information within the retention/use period required by law or agreed by the data subject at the time of collection.
bullets: 1. Website membership registration/management: until account withdrawal (except where investigation is ongoing or claims/debts remain)
2. Goods/service provision: until supply and payment settlement are completed
3. Records under E-commerce Consumer Protection Act: ads 6 months, contracts/payment/supply 5 years, complaints/disputes 3 years
4. Records under Protection of Communications Secrets Act: telecom fact records 1 year, internet log/access trace records 3 months

sections.title: Article 3 (Provision to Third Parties)
paragraphs: HanaLoop provides personal information to third parties only where permitted under Articles 17 and 18 of the Personal Information Protection Act, including data subject consent or specific legal grounds.
bullets: Example transfer items: name, date of birth, phone number, address

sections.title: Article 4 (Outsourcing of Processing)
paragraphs: HanaLoop may outsource personal information processing tasks for smooth business operations.
bullets: Identity verification service: Auth0.com

sections.title: Article 5 (Rights of Data Subjects and Legal Representatives)
bullets: Data subjects may request access, correction, deletion, or suspension of processing at any time.
Requests may be made in writing or by email, and HanaLoop will act without delay.
Requests may be made via a legal representative or authorized agent.
Access/suspension rights may be restricted under applicable law.
Deletion may be limited if data must be collected under other laws.
HanaLoop verifies whether the requester is the data subject or a legitimate representative.

sections.title: Article 6 (Items of Personal Information Processed)
bullets: 1. Website membership registration/management: required (name, ID, password, phone, email), optional (address, interests)
2. Service provision: required (name, ID, password, phone, email, credit card, bank account), optional (address, interests)
3. Automatically collected during internet use: IP address, cookies, MAC address, usage logs, visit history

sections.title: Article 7 (Destruction Procedure and Method)
paragraphs: HanaLoop destroys personal information without delay when the processing purpose is achieved, unless retention is required by other laws.
bullets: Electronic records: irreversibly deleted using technical methods
Paper records: shredded or incinerated

sections.title: Article 8 (Security Measures)
bullets: Administrative: internal management plan and regular employee training
Technical: access control, encryption of unique identifiers, security software
Physical: access control to server/data storage rooms

sections.title: Article 9 (Automatic Collection Devices and Refusal)
paragraphs: HanaLoop uses cookies to provide personalized services.
bullets: Purpose: analyze visit/usage patterns, popular searches, and secure access status for optimized information
Users can allow/block cookies in browser settings (Internet Explorer, Edge, Chrome).
Blocking cookies may cause difficulties in using services.

sections.title: Article 10 (Chief Privacy Officer)
paragraphs: HanaLoop designates the following person responsible for personal information protection and grievance handling.
bullets: Name: Hyeyeon Kim
Title: CEO
Contact: info@hanaloop.com

sections.title: Article 12 (Domestic Representative)
paragraphs: HanaLoop has designated a domestic representative under Article 39-11 of the Personal Information Protection Act.
bullets: Name: Hyeyeon Kim
Email: info@hanaloop.com

sections.title: Article 13 (Criteria for Additional Use/Provision)
paragraphs: When determining whether personal information can be additionally used/provided without consent under applicable law, HanaLoop considers:
bullets: Relevance to original collection purpose
Predictability based on collection circumstances/practice
Whether data subject interests are unfairly infringed
Whether safeguards such as pseudonymization/encryption are in place

sections.title: Article 14 (Request for Access)
paragraphs: Data subjects may request access to personal information under Article 35 of the Personal Information Protection Act through the department below.
bullets: Department: Development Support Division
Officer: Hyeyeon Kim
Contact: info@hanaloop.com

sections.title: Article 15 (Remedies for Infringement of Rights)
paragraphs: Data subjects may seek dispute resolution or consultation through the following organizations.
bullets: Personal Information Infringement Report Center (KISA): privacy.kisa.or.kr / 118
Personal Information Dispute Mediation Committee: www.kopico.go.kr / 1833-6972
Supreme Prosecutors' Office Cyber Bureau: www.spo.go.kr / 1301
National Police Agency Cyber Bureau: ecrm.cyber.go.kr / 182
Central Administrative Appeals Commission: www.simpan.go.kr / 110

sections.title: Article 16 (Changes to this Policy)
bullets: Effective date: September 1, 2022
Version: v0.8
Revision date: September 1, 2022

items.title: Doosan Enerbility × HanaLoop — Building a Supply Chain Carbon Management System

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: The existing internal carbon management system could not secure product-level supply chain carbon data including supplier information required for CBAM response.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop customized DPCM based on the Hana.eco license and linked it with the existing DPCF system to design an end-to-end flow from supplier data collection to product-level SEE calculation and automated CBAM reporting.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Doosan secured product-level carbon management across the supply chain and reduced CBAM compliance risk by automating manual reporting.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: Doosan Enerbility had already operated greenhouse gas systems for ETS since 2009 and advanced its product carbon footprint platform. CBAM, however, required a structural expansion toward supplier data and product-level embedded emissions including Scope 3.
Instead of building everything from scratch, Doosan chose to extend a proven solution quickly by adopting Hana.eco and connecting it with the existing DPCF system.

sections.title: Implementation — Designing the Data Flow
paragraphs: HanaLoop approached the project as carbon data infrastructure for the full supply chain, not as a simple software delivery.
bullets: Designed supplier data input, evidence management, and automated CBAM notification flows.
Mapped supplier data to products and connected it with process data for SEE calculation.
Embedded CBAM calculation logic and automated report generation in the system.
Supported supplier training and onboarding for practical operation.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: Doosan expanded from Scope 1 and 2 management to supplier-inclusive Scope 3, removed spreadsheet-based manual work, and secured both operating efficiency and data reliability.

items.title: Byucksan × HanaLoop — Carbon Management for Emissions Trading Compliance

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: Emissions data management and emissions trading reporting across multiple sites were concentrated on practitioners, creating repetitive manual work and making forecasting and reduction planning difficult.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop introduced Hana.eco Standard to integrate multi-site emissions data into one platform and build K-ETS-based emissions forecasting.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Byucksan standardized site-level emissions management and reporting, creating a data-driven operating system for emissions trading compliance.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: Byucksan manufactures building materials such as insulation, gypsum board, and cement. Because it operates multiple manufacturing sites, consistent site-level emissions management and K-ETS reporting had become a continuing operational burden.
The company needed a partner that could consolidate multi-site data and build a predictable carbon management process.

sections.title: HanaLoop Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop designed a structure that integrates site activity data and emissions data through Hana.eco Standard, moving from report-centered response to forecastable operations.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: Byucksan standardized dispersed emissions data and reduced repetitive manual work, enabling emissions trading reports and reduction planning to use the same data foundation.

items.title: CSWind × HanaLoop — Building a Company-wide CBAM Response System for Overseas Operations

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: With inadequate CBAM data management at overseas subsidiaries in Southeast Asia and the Middle East, and no company-wide response process or role structure in place at headquarters, it was difficult to guarantee continued EU export viability.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop supported end-to-end: CBAM data review and notification drafting for each subsidiary, company-wide process and R&R design, a certificate cost simulation tool, and ongoing advisory credit contracts — from subsidiary-level diagnosis to group-wide integrated response.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Established a company-wide climate regulation response process and passed a local third-party pre-verification, securing CBAM response credibility and export competitiveness in the EU market.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: CSWind is a leading Korean wind tower manufacturer, supplying products to the global renewable energy market through overseas subsidiaries in Vietnam and Turkey. Steel-based wind towers fall directly under EU CBAM regulation, making CBAM compliance directly tied to export continuity for CSWind, which has high EU export exposure.

The challenge was fragmented accountability. The Vietnam and Turkey subsidiaries each managed production data locally, but could not meet the data quality standards and embedded emission calculation methods required by CBAM. Without a company-wide response process and role structure at headquarters, the compliance gap was set to widen as regulatory requirements became more concrete.
CSWind chose HanaLoop as its partner to systematically build both the data management infrastructure at overseas subsidiaries and a company-wide CBAM response process.

sections.title: Implementation — From Subsidiary Diagnosis to Company-wide Process Design
paragraphs: HanaLoop designed this project not as isolated data checks for individual subsidiaries, but as an integrated effort to build CBAM response capabilities across the entire CSWind group.
bullets: Reviewed CBAM data quality and drafted Communication Templates for each subsidiary's representative products; assessed data gaps and produced improvement action lists per entity.
Redesigned the group-wide CBAM response flow from AS-IS to TO-BE; produced an R&R Matrix, draft CBAM operating rules, and internal communication and approval procedures.
Developed a cost simulation tool based on EU benchmark values and CBAM factors, enabling CSWind to forecast financial impact and inform strategic decisions ahead of full enforcement.
Established an ongoing advisory credit structure so CSWind can continue to respond to regulatory changes after project completion.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: Through this project, CSWind established both subsidiary-level data management and a headquarters-led company-wide CBAM response process. The core outcome was building a structure where regulatory response is managed at the group level rather than dispersed as individual subsidiary tasks.

items.title: DAEHO AL × HanaLoop — Building CBAM Carbon Calculation Infrastructure

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: As an SME producing aluminum products subject to CBAM, DAEHO AL needed product-level carbon calculation and verification capabilities for EU exports despite limited metering infrastructure and internal resources.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop built a Hana.eco-based carbon calculation system, designed field metering infrastructure, supported LRQA pre-verification, and trained internal staff end to end.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: DAEHO AL passed LRQA pre-verification and established company-wide CBAM processes, securing trusted data and calculation infrastructure for the EU market.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: DAEHO AL produces aluminum-based products, so CBAM compliance is directly tied to export continuity. It needed a system capable of producing reliable product-level emissions data and verification-ready evidence.

sections.title: Implementation Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop built a Hana.eco-based product-level calculation system, designed field metering infrastructure, and supported LRQA pre-verification documentation and response preparation.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: DAEHO AL gained company-wide CBAM operating processes and confirmed the reliability of its EU market response data through pre-verification.

items.title: Apollo Industrial × HanaLoop — Enterprise Carbon Inventory and ESG Management System

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: Apollo needed to respond to global customer ESG requirements and EcoVadis assessments, but lacked an enterprise GHG inventory across Scope 1, 2, and 3 and ERP-linked data processes.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop is building Scope 1, 2, and 3 inventories based on Hana.eco Standard, automating carbon data collection through ERP integration, and consulting on ESG management processes.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Apollo is improving data reliability through enterprise carbon management and ERP-based automation while building capabilities for global supply chain ESG requirements.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: Apollo Industrial produces plastic containers for global consumer brands. As ESG performance became a key supplier selection criterion, carbon management became a practical export competitiveness issue.

sections.title: Implementation Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop is building Scope 1, 2, and 3 inventories and linking production, purchasing, energy, and logistics data with Hana.eco through ERP integration while also designing ESG processes for assessment response.

sections.title: Expected Outcomes
paragraphs: Apollo is building the foundation to continuously secure reliable carbon data, respond to EcoVadis assessments, and meet global customer supply chain ESG requirements.

items.title: Kowell × HanaLoop — Enterprise ESG Data Integration and CBAM Infrastructure

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: Kowell needed to respond to EU CBAM and global customer ESG data requests, but lacked field metering infrastructure and integrated enterprise data management.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop is building field metering infrastructure based on Hana.eco CBAM & API and extending Kowell’s ERP into the carbon management domain through Hana.eco API integration.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Kowell is securing reliable measured data for CBAM and establishing enterprise carbon management processes inside its existing ERP environment.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: Kowell manufactures automotive parts and steel wire. Steel-based products are directly subject to EU CBAM, making carbon calculation and reporting essential for continued EU supply.

sections.title: Implementation Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop built field metering infrastructure, embedded CBAM calculation and reporting standards in the system, and designed an ERP integration structure that lets users view carbon management data inside the existing ERP.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: Kowell established measured-data infrastructure and enterprise ESG data integration, securing regulation-ready data and an internal operating foundation through practitioner training.

items.title: Korea Institute of Origin Information × HanaLoop x OpusM — FTA-PASS EU CBAM Management System

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: Korean manufacturers and exporters needed product-level emissions calculation and CBAM reporting, but many could not build their own systems, so CBAM functions had to be integrated into FTA-PASS.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop integrated its certified CBAM solution license into FTA-PASS, enabling emissions calculation and automated CBAM report generation inside the platform.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Around 30,000 FTA-PASS users can manage origin data and CBAM carbon data in one platform without building separate systems.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: Korea Institute of Origin Information operates FTA-PASS under Korea Customs Service oversight. EU CBAM created new requirements for Korean manufacturers and exporters to calculate product emissions and submit compliant reports, but many companies lacked internal systems.

sections.title: Implementation Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop integrated its certified CBAM solution license into FTA-PASS, implementing emissions calculation based on business and coefficient data and automated CBAM report generation inside the platform flow.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: FTA-PASS expanded from origin management to CBAM carbon management, allowing companies to access regulatory response infrastructure without building separate systems.

items.title: Taihan Fiber Optics (TFO Networks) × HanaLoop — Digital MRV and Carbon Cost Forecasting for CBAM

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: Customer requests for CBAM carbon data were increasing, but the company lacked field metering infrastructure, reliable product-level emissions calculation, and a way to forecast the financial impact of carbon costs.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop built field metering infrastructure, established a Hana.eco-based digital MRV system, created a CBAM certificate cost forecasting model, and supported pre-verification.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: Taihan gained infrastructure for ongoing customer data response, carbon cost forecasting, and EU market competitiveness through successful pre-verification.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: Taihan Fiber Optics supplies optical cable and network infrastructure products globally. It needed trusted product-level emissions data for customers and a way to understand CBAM certificate cost impacts before full enforcement.

sections.title: Implementation Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop installed meters at production sites, completed a digital MRV flow that automatically collects field data into Hana.eco, established CBAM calculation and documentation standards, and supported cost forecasting and pre-verification.

sections.title: Results
paragraphs: Taihan can now calculate product-level emissions from measured data and respond consistently to customer requests. Pre-verification also confirmed the reliability of its EU market response data.

items.title: emart × HanaLoop API Management Modernization

summary.label: Challenge
summary.text: emart faced limits in maintainability and operations due to degraded API integration quality and a lack of API management across internal systems such as eMoney.

summary.label: Solution
summary.text: HanaLoop sequentially performed API diagnosis, OpenAPI-based documentation repository setup, and an API Gateway PoC using Agile Scrum.

summary.label: Result
summary.text: The project identified API quality gaps, delivered improvement recommendations, established a standardized documentation repository, and validated integration through the Gateway to prepare the execution foundation for next-generation APIs.

sections.title: Project Background
paragraphs: emart’s core systems, including the eMoney payment platform, had grown independently for years. API quality gaps, missing documentation, and fragmented management made it difficult for users and integration teams to understand and operate APIs consistently.
emart selected HanaLoop because it needed a systematic path from diagnosis to standardization and validation, not simply a tool deployment.

sections.title: HanaLoop Approach
paragraphs: HanaLoop designed a three-stage engagement in which each deliverable became the input for the next stage, applying Agile Scrum with customer demo and review cycles to reduce delivery risk.

sections.title: Phase 1 — API Diagnosis
paragraphs: HanaLoop evaluated eMoney APIs across maintainability, integration, and operability, cross-checking documents, interviews, repositories, tests, and monitoring reports to identify structural quality gaps.

sections.title: Phase 2 — API Documentation Repository
paragraphs: HanaLoop defined eMoney API endpoints in OpenAPI format and built a documentation repository with SCM-linked automation so documentation could stay updated as APIs changed.

sections.title: Phase 3 — API Gateway PoC
paragraphs: HanaLoop installed the selected API Gateway, built a test client and mock server, and validated eMoney API integration including routing, caching, and rate limiting to demonstrate production feasibility.
